calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition For Order Authorizing Preservation Of Evidence
Off Calendar Feb-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):

Atty. Elizabeth Fiekowsky, Esq. of Emerald Law Group (Tel: 707-468-8300) for Petititioner
Attn. Maria Lampasona, Esq of RANKIN, SHUEY, RANUCCI, MINTZ, LAMPASONA & REYNOLDS (Tele: 510-433-2600) for Respondent Marriott International, Inc.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER DONNA ROSES' Notice Of Petition For Order Authorizing Preservation Of Evidence

Both parties stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and both counselors authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter discussed. The Court rule as follows, the tentative ruling is adopted:

The Petition is denied as follows: The Code of Civil Procedure section 2035.030(a) requires that a petition to preserve evidence be verified. Petitioner here failed to do so. Moreover, Marriot contends that the requested evidence has already been preserved. Since Petitioner was an eyewitness to the accident, she has standing to file a complaint. Nothing in this order shall be deemed to hold that any of Marriot's other objections are valid.

The moving party is required to prepare a proposed order(s) repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, as to form and in Word Format, and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Thomas Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: R. Sahatjian; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Petition For Order Authorizing Preservation Of Evidence
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Continued To Feb-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER DONNA ROSES' Petition For Order Authorizing Preservation Of Evidence.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is continued to be heard on the court's discovery calendar (room 301) at 9:00 a.m. on February 10, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
